phases.result:
Planning pipeline complete. The Story is persisted and `agent::ready`.

## Summary

**Planned:** [Story #1 — Minimal Node.js ESM Hello World HTTP server](https://github.com/dsj1984/bench-sbx-2026-07-24-hello-world-mandrel-57541177/issues/1)

**Pipeline run (headless, `--yes`):**
- **Interrogate** — envelope written; 0 duplicate Stories, no risk-heuristic hits. Gate #1 auto-proceeded.
- **Author** — one cohesive Story (single capability, no split warranted).
- **Critics** — consolidation & pre-mortem both skipped (ledgered: draft ≤5 stories, under budget, no external-dependency markers); text-hygiene clean.
- **Persist** — dry-run caught a file-assumption mismatch (`package.json` already exists → corrected `creates` → `refactors-existing`); re-run passed all gates; real persist created the issue, terminal-flipped to `agent::ready`.

**Story scope:**
- **Goal:** dependency-free Node.js `http` entry point serving a Hello World page.
- **Contract:** ESM, listens on `PORT` (default 3000), `GET /` → 200 / `Content-Type: text/html` / body containing exact `Hello, World!`; `start` script runs the single source file; one source file (`server.js`) + `package.json` only.
- **Verify:** an `e2e` curl check of status/header/body and a `node --check` validate step.
- Cohort label: `plan-run::6bb3f3bd`. Deliver later with `/deliver 1`.

As instructed, I ran **only** the planning pipeline — no delivery, no pre-staged implementation artifacts.

`landed`. Story #1 delivered end-to-end. This was a single-Story run, so no per-run epilogue.

Terminal envelope (relayed verbatim):

```json
{"kind":"story-deliver-terminal","storyId":1,"status":"landed","phase":"post-land","storyBranch":"story-1","baseBranch":"main","pr":{"number":2,"url":"https://github.com/dsj1984/bench-sbx-2026-07-24-hello-world-mandrel-57541177/pull/2","autoMergeEnabled":true,"state":"MERGED","checksStatus":"success"},"gates":{"validation":"passed","baseSync":"passed","codeReview":"passed"},"tail":{"followUps":true,"statusResync":true,"refCleanup":true,"baseFastForward":false,"details":{"baseFastForward":"fast-forward skipped: dirty-tree"}},"nextCommand":null,"elapsedSeconds":97}
```

**Summary**
- **Story #1** — Minimal Node.js ESM Hello World HTTP server — **landed** via PR [#2](https://github.com/dsj1984/bench-sbx-2026-07-24-hello-world-mandrel-57541177/pull/2), squash-merged to `main`, `agent::done`.
- **Implementation** — `server.js` (built-in `node:http` only) + `package.json` (`start` script added; already `"type": "module"`, zero runtime deps).
- **Acceptance self-eval** — 4/4 criteria `met`, decision `proceed`.
- **Verify** — `node --check` passed; e2e curl confirmed HTTP 200, `Content-Type: text/html`, body containing `Hello, World!`; default port 3000 and `PORT=3100` both honored.
- **Close gates** — validation, base-sync, code review all passed (0 findings). Format autofix committed. Worktree reaped.

One benign degradation: the post-land fast-forward of local `main` was skipped because the working tree is dirty (the pre-existing untracked `temp/`); the merge itself stands and required no action.
